templates.title: Daily Scheduled Restart
templates.description: Reboots the device once a day at a configurable hour by attaching a schedule to an existing time component.

vars.name: st_restart_hour
vars.description: Hour of day (0-23, local time) at which to restart

vars.name: st_daily_restart_time_id
vars.description: id of the time component to attach the schedule to (st_time_ha for the HA time preset)

notes: Needs a time: component. Include core/time_sntp.yaml (default) or core/time_homeassistant.yaml (then pass st_daily_restart_time_id: st_time_ha). ESPHome allows only one SNTP instance, so this file deliberately does NOT ship its own clock - it attaches to yours via !extend.

time.on_time does not reschedule across DST jumps - an event in the 01:00-02:00 window may be skipped or fire twice on the changeover day. Keeping the default 03:00-04:00 window is safe in most zones.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_restart_hour: "4"
  st_daily_restart_time_id: st_time_sntp

time:
  # Attach the schedule to an already-included time component (plain
  # re-declaration of an id is a hard "ID redefined!" error - !extend is the
  # sanctioned way for a package to add options to another package's entry).
  - id: !extend ${st_daily_restart_time_id}
    on_time:
      - hours: ${st_restart_hour}
        minutes: 0
        seconds: 0
        then:
          - button.press: st_daily_restart_button

button:
  # Always internal: this is a scheduling primitive, not a user control. Kept as an entity (with a
  # name) rather than a bare action so web_server's include_internal can still surface/trigger it.
  - platform: restart
    id: st_daily_restart_button
    name: "Daily Restart"
    entity_category: config
    icon: mdi:restart
    device_class: restart
    internal: true

templates.title: Factory Reset Button
templates.description: Exposes a button that erases all stored preferences and returns the device to its fresh-flash state. Ships disabled by default because an accidental press is destructive.

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA (overrides the library default - see note)

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

notes: IRREVERSIBLE. Erases all stored preferences - WiFi credentials, restore_value globals (e.g. boot counter), and improv/captive-portal provisioning - and returns the device to a fresh-flash state. It will not reconnect until re-provisioned.
Ships disabled_by_default: true (unlike the rest of the library) so an accidental tap cannot wipe the device; the user must deliberately enable it in HA.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_name_prefix: ""
  # Override of the shared default: this control is destructive, so it ships disabled in HA.
  st_disabled_by_default: "true"
  st_internal: "false"

button:
  - platform: factory_reset
    id: st_factory_reset_button
    name: "${st_name_prefix}Factory Reset"
    entity_category: config
    icon: mdi:delete-restore
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}

templates.title: Restart Button
templates.description: Exposes a button in HA/web that soft-reboots the device (equivalent to a normal power cycle, config is re-applied).

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

notes: A plain restart re-applies the full config on next boot; use safe_mode_button.yaml instead if a broken config is preventing a normal boot.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"

button:
  - platform: restart
    id: st_restart_button
    name: "${st_name_prefix}Restart"
    entity_category: config
    icon: mdi:restart
    device_class: restart
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}

templates.title: Safe Mode Button
templates.description: Exposes a button that reboots the device into safe mode, where only network/logging/OTA run so a broken config can be re-flashed.

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

notes: Safe-mode boot brings up only network, logging and OTA - the rest of your config is skipped - so a config that crashes on boot can still be re-flashed over the air.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"

# Bare safe_mode: so the safe_mode button platform has something to trigger. Declared with no
# options here: if core/safe_mode.yaml is also included, ESPHome deep-merges the two dicts
# key-by-key, so its tuned thresholds win and this bare block adds nothing.
safe_mode:

button:
  - platform: safe_mode
    id: st_safe_mode_button
    name: "${st_name_prefix}Restart (Safe Mode)"
    entity_category: config
    icon: mdi:restart-alert
    device_class: restart
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}

templates.title: Shutdown Button
templates.description: Exposes a button that shuts the device down into deep sleep with no wake source. Only a physical reset or power cycle brings it back.

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

notes: Enters deep sleep with no wake source configured - the device stops responding to WiFi/API/OTA. Only a physical reset button press or power cycle recovers it, so do not press this on a device you cannot reach.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"

button:
  - platform: shutdown
    id: st_shutdown_button
    name: "${st_name_prefix}Shutdown"
    entity_category: config
    icon: mdi:power
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}

templates.title: Status LED
templates.description: Drives an on-board LED as a firmware status indicator - slow blink on warnings (WiFi/API down), fast blink on errors.

vars.name: st_status_led_pin
vars.description: GPIO the status LED is wired to (e.g. GPIO8)

vars.name: st_status_led_inverted
vars.description: C++ literal "true"/"false" - set "true" for active-low LEDs (many on-board LEDs)

notes: Blink semantics are fixed by ESPHome and cannot be customized - slow blink = warning (wifi/api down), fast blink = error. There is no HA entity; the LED reflects internal firmware state only.
The pin is claimed exclusively by status_led and is otherwise unusable for other outputs.

templates.title: Home Assistant API
templates.description: Native Home Assistant API preset with encryption and a survival-friendly reboot_timeout. Provides the api: component templates like time_homeassistant require.

vars.name: st_api_reboot_timeout
vars.description: How long the API may stay disconnected before the device reboots (0s = never)

vars.name: st_api_key
vars.description: Base64 API encryption key; pass via vars: { st_api_key: !secret api_key }

notes: Never store the key in this file - it is a required var so the user supplies it with their own !secret.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_api_reboot_timeout: 0s

api:
  # reboot_timeout defaults to 15min in ESPHome. With any short timeout the
  # device reboot-loops whenever Home Assistant is unreachable longer than the
  # window - HA restarts, HA OS updates, add-on upgrades - and every reboot
  # wipes in-RAM state (uptime, un-persisted globals, sensor history). Setting
  # 0s means "never reboot just because the API is down": the device keeps
  # running standalone and stays usable via web_server until HA returns.
  reboot_timeout: ${st_api_reboot_timeout}
  encryption:
    # Required var - no default. Users pass: vars: { st_api_key: !secret api_key }
    key: ${st_api_key}

templates.title: Logger
templates.description: Logger preset with configurable level and UART baud rate.

vars.name: st_log_level
vars.description: Log verbosity (compile-time ceiling)

vars.name: st_log_baud_rate
vars.description: UART baud rate; set 0 to disable UART (serial) logging entirely

notes: Since ESPHome 2026.4.0 state-change messages are logged at VERBOSE, so INFO/DEBUG stays cheap - the noisy per-state spam no longer floods those levels.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_log_level: INFO
  st_log_baud_rate: "115200"

logger:
  level: ${st_log_level}
  # Set st_log_baud_rate to 0 to turn off UART logging (frees the serial pins,
  # saves the cost of formatting/writing every line to the port).
  baud_rate: ${st_log_baud_rate}

templates.title: OTA Updates
templates.description: Over-the-air update preset (esphome platform) with a password gate and progress logging hooks.

vars.name: st_ota_password
vars.description: OTA upload password; pass via vars: { st_ota_password: !secret ota_password }

notes: Presence of ota: also auto-enables safe_mode. Include safe_mode.yaml only to tune its thresholds.
templates.body:
ota:
  - platform: esphome
    # Required var - no default. Users pass: vars: { st_ota_password: !secret ota_password }
    password: ${st_ota_password}
    on_begin:
      then:
        - logger.log: "OTA start"
    on_progress:
      then:
        - logger.log:
            format: "OTA progress %0.1f%%"
            args: ["x"]
    on_end:
      then:
        - logger.log: "OTA end"
    on_error:
      then:
        - logger.log:
            format: "OTA update error %d"
            args: ["x"]

templates.title: Safe Mode
templates.description: Tunes the boot-loop recovery safe_mode: after repeated crash-boots the device stops applying its config so an OTA fix can land.

vars.name: st_safe_mode_attempts
vars.description: Consecutive failed boots before entering safe mode

vars.name: st_safe_mode_reboot_timeout
vars.description: How long to wait in safe mode for an OTA before rebooting to retry

vars.name: st_safe_mode_boot_good_after
vars.description: Uptime after which a boot counts as successful and the attempt counter resets

notes: Required by the safe_mode button/switch platforms. safe_mode is auto-enabled anyway whenever ota: is present - this preset just tunes its thresholds.

templates.title: Home Assistant Time
templates.description: Time source synced from Home Assistant. Provides the time: component other templates (daily_restart, last_boot) require.
notes: Timezone auto-syncs from Home Assistant unless you set it in YAML - override with "time: - id: !extend st_time_ha / timezone: Europe/Berlin".
Do not include together with time_sntp.yaml unless you want two time sources.
templates.body:
time:
  - platform: homeassistant
    id: st_time_ha

templates.title: SNTP Time
templates.description: SNTP time source with configurable server. Provides the time: component other templates (daily_restart, last_boot) require.

vars.name: st_ntp_server
vars.description: NTP server to sync against

notes: Timezone is deliberately NOT set here - ESPHome auto-infers it from the build machine. Override per device with "time: - id: !extend st_time_sntp / timezone: Europe/Berlin".
Do not include together with time_homeassistant.yaml unless you want two time sources.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_ntp_server: 0.pool.ntp.org

time:
  - platform: sntp
    id: st_time_sntp
    servers:
      - ${st_ntp_server}
    on_time_sync:
      then:
        - logger.log: "Synchronized system clock"

templates.title: Web Server
templates.description: Built-in web UI (v3) with HTTP basic auth and a boot-time gate that can disable auth without editing the config. Keeps the device usable standalone when Home Assistant is down.

vars.name: st_web_include_internal
vars.description: Show internal: entities in the web UI too (C++ bool literal)

vars.name: st_web_username
vars.description: HTTP basic auth username; MUST NOT be empty (see note)

vars.name: st_web_password
vars.description: HTTP basic auth password; pass via vars: { st_web_password: !secret web_password }

vars.name: st_web_auth
vars.description: Enable auth at boot - must be exactly "true" or "false", substituted as a C++ bool literal

notes: st_web_auth is injected raw into a lambda as a C++ bool literal, so it MUST be exactly "true" or "false" - not "1", "yes", or "True".

WebServerBase::add_handler only wraps handlers in auth middleware when credentials are non-empty AT REGISTRATION TIME. web_server registers its handlers in setup() at priority ~249 (WIFI-1), so clearing the credentials in on_boot at priority 600 (which runs earlier) disables auth for the whole boot.

st_web_username must NOT be empty - web_server_idf's authenticate() FAILS OPEN on an empty username (returns true for every request), silently disabling auth. Password-only auth is therefore not possible.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_web_include_internal: "true"
  st_web_username: "admin"
  st_web_auth: "true"

esphome:
  on_boot:
    # Priority 600 runs before web_server registers its handlers (setup, ~249),
    # so clearing credentials here means add_handler never wraps them in auth
    # middleware for this boot. See @note above for the timing details.
    - priority: 600
      then:
        - lambda: |-
            if (!${st_web_auth}) {
              web_server_base::global_web_server_base->set_auth_username("");
              web_server_base::global_web_server_base->set_auth_password("");
            }

web_server:
  version: 3
  include_internal: ${st_web_include_internal}
  auth:
    # Username must stay non-empty even when auth is "disabled" - an empty
    # username makes authenticate() fail open (see @note above).
    username: "${st_web_username}"
    # Required var - no default. Users pass: vars: { st_web_password: !secret web_password }
    password: "${st_web_password}"

templates.title: Boot Counter
templates.description: Counts device boots in an NVS-backed global and publishes the running total. Rising counts between expected reboots point at brown-outs, watchdog resets, or crashes.

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

notes: One NVS write per boot (restore_value) - negligible flash wear. The count survives reboots and OTA but NOT a factory_reset or a wired flash-erase.
This is a counter - state_class is hardcoded total_increasing and does NOT honor the ${st_state_class} knob. There is no update_interval: the value is published once from on_boot.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"

# NVS-backed counter. restore_value persists it across reboots/OTA.
globals:
  - id: st_boot_counter_value
    type: int
    restore_value: true
    initial_value: "0"

esphome:
  on_boot:
    # priority 600 runs early, before Wi-Fi/API, so the count is ready to publish.
    - priority: 600
      then:
        - lambda: |-
            id(st_boot_counter_value) += 1;
            id(st_boot_counter).publish_state(id(st_boot_counter_value));

sensor:
  - platform: template
    id: st_boot_counter
    name: "${st_name_prefix}Boot Count"
    # Published only from on_boot; never polled.
    update_interval: never
    accuracy_decimals: 0
    # Monotonic counter: total_increasing is required, hardcoded (no knob).
    state_class: total_increasing
    icon: mdi:counter
    entity_category: diagnostic
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}

templates.title: Chip Info
templates.description: Reports the SoC model, silicon revision, core count and radio features decoded from esp_chip_info(). One glance confirms exactly which ESP32 variant a build is running on.

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

notes: Static per boot - update_interval is never and the value is published exactly once from on_boot at priority -100 (after sensors are set up).
On IDF5 esp_chip_info_t.revision is encoded as major*100+minor, so it is split back into "major.minor" (e.g. 0.4). The features bitmask is decoded to a "/"-joined radio list (WiFi/BT/BLE/802.15.4).

templates.title: CPU Frequency
templates.description: Reports the current CPU clock from the debug component. Useful for confirming power-save/DFS behaviour or a mis-set framework clock.

vars.name: st_update_interval
vars.description: How often the debug hub refreshes the clock

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

vars.name: st_state_class
vars.description: state_class for the sensor; set "" to opt out of HA long-term statistics

notes: Shares the debug: hub with the other debug-based templates; dicts merge across packages and the last update_interval wins.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_update_interval: 60s
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"
  st_state_class: measurement

# Shared debug hub (merges with other debug-based templates).
debug:
  update_interval: ${st_update_interval}

sensor:
  - platform: debug
    # The debug component reports the clock in Hz, so unit is Hz and it is a
    # whole number - accuracy_decimals 0.
    cpu_frequency:
      id: st_cpu_frequency
      name: "${st_name_prefix}CPU Frequency"
      device_class: frequency
      unit_of_measurement: Hz
      accuracy_decimals: 0
      state_class: "${st_state_class}"
      entity_category: diagnostic
      disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
      internal: ${st_internal}
      icon: mdi:speedometer

templates.title: Device Info
templates.description: Exposes the debug component's device text sensors - a one-line hardware/firmware summary (chip model, cores, revision, flash size, ESPHome version) and the last reset reason.

vars.name: st_update_interval
vars.description: How often the debug hub refreshes the text sensors

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to every entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entities disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entities from HA/web entirely

notes: Prefer this over hand-rolled esp_reset_reason() lambdas - the debug component decodes the reason string for you.
Shares the debug: hub with the other debug-based templates; dicts merge across packages and the last update_interval wins.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_update_interval: 60s
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"

# Shared debug hub (merges with other debug-based templates).
debug:
  update_interval: ${st_update_interval}

text_sensor:
  - platform: debug
    # device: is the multi-field hardware/firmware summary; reset_reason: is the
    # decoded cause of the last restart (both text - no state_class).
    device:
      id: st_device_info
      name: "${st_name_prefix}Device Info"
      entity_category: diagnostic
      disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
      internal: ${st_internal}
      icon: mdi:chip
    reset_reason:
      id: st_reset_reason
      name: "${st_name_prefix}Reset Reason"
      entity_category: diagnostic
      disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
      internal: ${st_internal}
      icon: mdi:restart

templates.title: ESPHome Version
templates.description: Publishes the ESPHome version the firmware was built with via the version text_sensor platform. Handy for spotting devices that missed an OTA.

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

vars.name: st_version_hide_timestamp
vars.description: "true" drops the compile timestamp and shows just the version string (C++ literal)

notes: The value is fixed at compile time, so it only changes on a rebuild/OTA.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"
  st_version_hide_timestamp: "false"

text_sensor:
  - platform: version
    id: st_esphome_version
    name: "${st_name_prefix}ESPHome Version"
    icon: mdi:chip
    entity_category: diagnostic
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}
    # true = show just the version, false = append the build timestamp.
    hide_timestamp: ${st_version_hide_timestamp}

templates.title: Flash Size
templates.description: Reports the physical SPI flash size in MiB, read from the flash chip at runtime via esp_flash_get_size(). Confirms whether a board really carries the 4/8/16 MiB it was sold as.

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

notes: Static per boot - update_interval is never and the value is published exactly once from on_boot at priority -100 (after sensors are set up).
No state_class is set - a fixed hardware size is not a measurement and needs no HA long-term statistics, so it deliberately does NOT honor the ${st_state_class} knob.
Flash SPEED and mode are compile-time board settings and are not exposed at runtime; the debug component's device_info text sensor shows them in its summary string.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"

esphome:
  # esp_flash.h is not pulled into main.cpp by any component, so force-include it
  # for the lambda below. build_src_flags only affects the project's own src/
  # (main.cpp) and the list concatenates cleanly with flags from other templates.
  platformio_options:
    build_src_flags:
      - "-include esp_flash.h"
  on_boot:
    # priority -100 runs late, after all sensors are set up, so the one-shot
    # component.update below publishes into a live entity.
    - priority: -100
      then:
        - component.update: st_flash_size

sensor:
  - platform: template
    id: st_flash_size
    name: "${st_name_prefix}Flash Size"
    unit_of_measurement: MiB
    device_class: data_size
    accuracy_decimals: 0
    icon: mdi:memory
    entity_category: diagnostic
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}
    # Static hardware fact - computed once and pushed from on_boot, never polled.
    update_interval: never
    # NULL selects esp_flash_default_chip (the on-board SPI flash).
    lambda: |-
      uint32_t size;
      if (esp_flash_get_size(NULL, &size) != ESP_OK) return NAN;
      return size / 1048576.0;

templates.title: Heap Diagnostics
templates.description: Reports internal heap health from the debug component - free bytes, largest allocatable block, historical minimum free, and fragmentation percent. The first thing to check when a device reboots under memory pressure.

vars.name: st_update_interval
vars.description: How often the debug hub refreshes heap stats

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to every entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entities disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entities from HA/web entirely

vars.name: st_state_class
vars.description: state_class for the numeric sensors; set "" to opt out of HA long-term statistics

notes: Several diagnostics templates declare the debug: hub. ESPHome deep-merges the dict across packages, so the last update_interval wins - keep the knob identical everywhere.
Sustained fragmentation above ~50% is risky - large contiguous allocations (TLS buffers, JSON) can fail even with free bytes available.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_update_interval: 60s
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"
  st_state_class: measurement

# Shared debug hub. Dicts merge across packages; the update_interval set by
# whichever debug-based template is merged last wins for all of them.
debug:
  update_interval: ${st_update_interval}

sensor:
  - platform: debug
    free:
      id: st_heap_free
      name: "${st_name_prefix}Heap Free"
      state_class: "${st_state_class}"
      entity_category: diagnostic
      disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
      internal: ${st_internal}
      icon: mdi:memory
    block:
      id: st_heap_max_block
      name: "${st_name_prefix}Heap Max Block"
      state_class: "${st_state_class}"
      entity_category: diagnostic
      disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
      internal: ${st_internal}
      icon: mdi:memory
    min_free:
      id: st_heap_min_free
      name: "${st_name_prefix}Heap Min Free"
      state_class: "${st_state_class}"
      entity_category: diagnostic
      disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
      internal: ${st_internal}
      icon: mdi:memory
    fragmentation:
      id: st_heap_fragmentation
      name: "${st_name_prefix}Heap Fragmentation"
      unit_of_measurement: "%"
      state_class: "${st_state_class}"
      entity_category: diagnostic
      disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
      internal: ${st_internal}
      icon: mdi:memory

templates.title: Internal Temperature
templates.description: Reads the on-die temperature sensor and derives a coupled overtemperature warning binary sensor that trips above a configurable threshold.

vars.name: st_update_interval
vars.description: How often to sample the die temperature

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to every entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entities disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entities from HA/web entirely

vars.name: st_state_class
vars.description: state_class for the temperature sensor; set "" to opt out of HA long-term statistics

vars.name: st_overtemp_threshold
vars.description: Warning trips above this many degC (numeric, substituted into C++)

notes: Some ESP32 variants report bogus values (e.g. a stuck 53.3 degC / raw 128). The internal_temperature component discards invalid readings, and the warning lambda ignores NaN.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_update_interval: 60s
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"
  st_state_class: measurement
  st_overtemp_threshold: "80"

sensor:
  - platform: internal_temperature
    id: st_internal_temperature
    name: "${st_name_prefix}Internal Temperature"
    device_class: temperature
    unit_of_measurement: "°C"
    accuracy_decimals: 1
    state_class: "${st_state_class}"
    entity_category: diagnostic
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}
    icon: mdi:thermometer
    update_interval: ${st_update_interval}

binary_sensor:
  - platform: template
    id: st_overtemp_warning
    name: "${st_name_prefix}Overtemperature"
    device_class: heat
    entity_category: diagnostic
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}
    icon: mdi:thermometer-alert
    # Return {} while the reading is unknown so we never latch a false alarm on
    # a NaN; ${st_overtemp_threshold} is a bare number spliced into the compare.
    lambda: |-
      if (isnan(id(st_internal_temperature).state)) return {};
      return id(st_internal_temperature).state > ${st_overtemp_threshold};

vars.description: state_class for the sensor; set "" to opt out of HA long-term statistics

notes: Shares the debug: hub with the other debug-based templates; dicts merge across packages and the last update_interval wins.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_update_interval: 60s
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"

  st_state_class: measurement

# Shared debug hub (merges with other debug-based templates).
debug:

templates.title: Memory Info
templates.description: Reports total installed internal RAM and total PSRAM in KiB, read from the heap capability registry. Confirms how much DRAM and external SPI RAM the running build actually sees.

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity names

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entities disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entities from HA/web entirely

notes: Static per boot - update_interval is never and both values are published exactly once from on_boot at priority -100 (after sensors are set up).
No state_class is set - fixed hardware totals are not measurements and need no HA long-term statistics, so they deliberately do NOT honor the ${st_state_class} knob.

PSRAM Total reports 0 on boards without external SPI RAM (e.g. ESP32-C3/C6). It reads the heap capability registry (MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM) directly and does NOT require the psram: component to be configured.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"

esphome:
  # esp_heap_caps.h is not pulled into main.cpp by any component, so force-include
  # it for the lambdas below. build_src_flags only affects the project's own src/
  # (main.cpp) and the list concatenates cleanly with flags from other templates.
  platformio_options:
    build_src_flags:
      - "-include esp_heap_caps.h"
  on_boot:
    # priority -100 runs late, after all sensors are set up, so the one-shot
    # component.updates below publish into live entities.
    - priority: -100
      then:
        - component.update: st_ram_total
        - component.update: st_psram_total

sensor:
  - platform: template
    id: st_ram_total
    name: "${st_name_prefix}RAM Total"
    unit_of_measurement: KiB
    device_class: data_size
    accuracy_decimals: 0
    icon: mdi:memory
    entity_category: diagnostic
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}
    # Static hardware fact - computed once and pushed from on_boot, never polled.
    update_interval: never
    lambda: |-
      return heap_caps_get_total_size(MALLOC_CAP_INTERNAL) / 1024.0;

  - platform: template
    id: st_psram_total
    name: "${st_name_prefix}PSRAM Total"
    unit_of_measurement: KiB
    device_class: data_size
    accuracy_decimals: 0
    icon: mdi:memory
    entity_category: diagnostic
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}
    # Static hardware fact - computed once and pushed from on_boot, never polled.
    # Reports 0 on boards with no PSRAM (registry has no MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM pool).
    update_interval: never
    lambda: |-
      return heap_caps_get_total_size(MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM) / 1024.0;

templates.title: NVS Usage
templates.description: Reports NVS entry fill (used/total) and the on-device NVS partition size. Diagnoses ESP_ERR_NVS_NOT_ENOUGH_SPACE before it bites.

vars.name: st_update_interval
vars.description: How often to poll NVS statistics

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

notes: OTA never updates the partition table. A device first flashed with an old ESPHome may still run a 20 kB NVS even though current ESPHome allocates 448 kB - only a wired flash replaces the table.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_update_interval: 60s
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"

esphome:
  # nvs.h / esp_partition.h are not pulled into main.cpp by any component, so
  # force-include them for the lambda below. build_src_flags only affects the
  # project's own src/ (main.cpp), not the framework build, and the list
  # concatenates cleanly with flags from other templates.
  platformio_options:
    build_src_flags:
      - "-include nvs.h"
      - "-include esp_partition.h"

text_sensor:
  - platform: template
    id: st_nvs_usage
    name: "${st_name_prefix}NVS Usage"
    icon: mdi:database
    entity_category: diagnostic
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}
    update_interval: ${st_update_interval}
    # nvs_get_stats() covers the default "nvs" partition; the partition lookup
    # reports the size actually flashed to THIS device (see @note above).
    lambda: |-
      nvs_stats_t s;
      if (nvs_get_stats(NULL, &s) != ESP_OK) return {"unavailable"};
      const esp_partition_t *p = esp_partition_find_first(
          ESP_PARTITION_TYPE_DATA, ESP_PARTITION_SUBTYPE_DATA_NVS, nullptr);
      char buf[64];
      snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "%u/%u entries, %u kB partition",
               (unsigned) s.used_entries, (unsigned) s.total_entries,
               p != nullptr ? (unsigned) (p->size / 1024) : 0);
      return {std::string(buf)};

templates.body:

templates.body:

templates.title: HA-Controlled Deep Sleep
templates.description: The classic Home Assistant-controlled deep sleep pattern. The device sleeps between runs but honors an HA input_boolean that can hold it awake for OTA/config.
requires: HA input_boolean helper named by st_prevent_sleep_entity

vars.name: st_sleep_duration
vars.description: How long the device stays asleep between runs

vars.name: st_run_duration
vars.description: How long the device stays awake each run before sleeping

vars.name: st_prevent_sleep_entity
vars.description: HA entity that, when ON, prevents sleep

notes: SINGLE-INSTANCE - this file uses fixed ids (st_deep_sleep, st_prevent_sleep). Include it exactly ONCE.
Requires an active api: connection - the homeassistant binary_sensor platform reads the toggle over the API.

The device can only see the toggle WHILE AWAKE. Flip the HA input_boolean ON *before* the next wake so the device catches it during its brief run_duration window (that window is your OTA opportunity).
When the toggle goes back OFF (on_release), deep_sleep.allow re-arms sleep and restarts the run_duration timer, so the device stays awake for a fresh run_duration before sleeping again.
Create the helper in HA: Settings > Devices & Services > Helpers > Toggle, matching st_prevent_sleep_entity.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_sleep_duration: 10min
  st_run_duration: 60s
  st_prevent_sleep_entity: input_boolean.prevent_deep_sleep

deep_sleep:
  id: st_deep_sleep
  sleep_duration: ${st_sleep_duration}
  run_duration: ${st_run_duration}

binary_sensor:
  # Always internal: this is a control input from HA, not a user-facing entity.
  - platform: homeassistant
    id: st_prevent_sleep
    entity_id: ${st_prevent_sleep_entity}
    internal: true
    # Toggle ON in HA -> hold the device awake.
    on_press:
      then:
        - deep_sleep.prevent: st_deep_sleep
    # Toggle OFF in HA -> re-allow sleep and restart the run_duration timer.
    on_release:
      then:
        - deep_sleep.allow: st_deep_sleep

templates.title: Generic Number Input
templates.description: A user-adjustable template number, surfaced in HA as a config control. Use it for thresholds, calibration offsets, setpoints - read the value elsewhere via id(...).state.

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

vars.name: st_number_id
vars.description: Entity id - MUST be overridden on every extra include (see notes)

vars.name: st_number_name
vars.description: Entity name (after st_name_prefix)

vars.name: st_number_min
vars.description: Minimum selectable value

vars.name: st_number_max
vars.description: Maximum selectable value

vars.name: st_number_step
vars.description: Increment between values

vars.name: st_number_initial
vars.description: Value used before anything is restored/set

vars.name: st_number_unit
vars.description: Unit of measurement shown in HA (e.g. "C", "%")

vars.name: st_number_mode
vars.description: UI control style in HA

vars.name: st_number_restore
vars.description: Persist the value across reboots (C++ literal "true"/"false")

notes: MULTI-INSTANCE - include this file more than once to get several numbers. Each extra include MUST override BOTH st_number_id AND st_number_name; duplicate ids are a hard "ID redefined!" compile error.
On a template number, restore_value is INCOMPATIBLE with a lambda, so this template uses optimistic:true (the entity holds its own state) and no lambda.
restore_value:true writes to NVS on every change (one flash write per change). NVS flash has limited write endurance - leave it "false" for values that change very often.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"
  st_number_id: st_generic_number
  st_number_name: "Setpoint"
  st_number_min: "-100"
  st_number_max: "100"
  st_number_step: "1"
  st_number_initial: "0"
  st_number_unit: ""
  st_number_mode: box
  st_number_restore: "true"

number:
  - platform: template
    # The id itself is a var so the file can be included multiple times.
    id: ${st_number_id}
    name: "${st_name_prefix}${st_number_name}"
    min_value: ${st_number_min}
    max_value: ${st_number_max}
    step: ${st_number_step}
    initial_value: ${st_number_initial}
    unit_of_measurement: "${st_number_unit}"
    mode: ${st_number_mode}
    # optimistic + no lambda: the component stores its own state.
    optimistic: true
    restore_value: ${st_number_restore}
    entity_category: config
    icon: mdi:tune
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}

templates.title: Generic Select Input
templates.description: A user-adjustable template select (dropdown), surfaced in HA as a config control. Use it to pick a mode/profile and read the choice elsewhere via id(...).state.

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

vars.name: st_select_id
vars.description: Entity id - MUST be overridden on every extra include (see notes)

vars.name: st_select_name
vars.description: Entity name (after st_name_prefix)

vars.name: st_select_options
vars.description: List of options - pass a YAML list in vars:

vars.name: st_select_initial
vars.description: Option selected before anything is restored/set

vars.name: st_select_restore
vars.description: Persist the selection across reboots (C++ literal "true"/"false")

notes: MULTI-INSTANCE - include this file more than once to get several selects. Each extra include MUST override BOTH st_select_id AND st_select_name; duplicate ids are a hard "ID redefined!" compile error.
st_select_options is a LIST-valued substitution. Pass it exactly like any other var, but as a YAML list, e.g. vars: { st_select_options: ["Eco", "Comfort", "Boost"] }.
st_select_initial MUST be one of st_select_options, otherwise config validation fails.
On a template select, restore_value is INCOMPATIBLE with a lambda, so this template uses optimistic:true (the entity holds its own state) and no lambda.
restore_value:true writes to NVS on every change (one flash write per change) - NVS flash has limited write endurance.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"
  st_select_id: st_generic_select
  st_select_name: "Mode"
  # List-valued substitution: users override by passing their own YAML list.
  st_select_options:
    - "Option A"
    - "Option B"
  st_select_initial: "Option A"
  st_select_restore: "true"

select:
  - platform: template
    # The id itself is a var so the file can be included multiple times.
    id: ${st_select_id}
    name: "${st_name_prefix}${st_select_name}"
    options: ${st_select_options}
    initial_option: "${st_select_initial}"
    # optimistic + no lambda: the component stores its own state.
    optimistic: true
    restore_value: ${st_select_restore}
    entity_category: config
    icon: mdi:form-dropdown
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}

templates.title: Generic Switch (virtual flag)
templates.description: A virtual template switch, surfaced in HA as a config control. Use it as an on/off flag to gate automations - read it via id(...).state or attach actions with !extend.

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

vars.name: st_switch_id
vars.description: Entity id - MUST be overridden on every extra include (see notes)

vars.name: st_switch_name
vars.description: Entity name (after st_name_prefix)

vars.name: st_switch_restore_mode
vars.description: Boot behavior - see enum in notes

notes: MULTI-INSTANCE - include this file more than once to get several switches. Each extra include MUST override BOTH st_switch_id AND st_switch_name; duplicate ids are a hard "ID redefined!" compile error.

restore_mode enum: RESTORE_DEFAULT_OFF, RESTORE_DEFAULT_ON, ALWAYS_OFF, ALWAYS_ON, RESTORE_INVERTED_DEFAULT_OFF, RESTORE_INVERTED_DEFAULT_ON, DISABLED. RESTORE_* variants read the last state from NVS (one flash write per change); ALWAYS_* never persist.
This is a virtual flag - it drives nothing on its own. Read id(st_generic_switch).state in a lambda, or attach on_turn_on/on_turn_off actions via "switch: - id: !extend st_generic_switch".
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"

  st_switch_name: "Flag"
  st_switch_restore_mode: RESTORE_DEFAULT_OFF

switch:

    # The id itself is a var so the file can be included multiple times.
    id: ${st_switch_id}
    name: "${st_name_prefix}${st_switch_name}"
    # optimistic: the switch tracks its own commanded state (no output to drive).
    optimistic: true
    restore_mode: ${st_switch_restore_mode}
    entity_category: config
    icon: mdi:toggle-switch-outline
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}

vars.description: Boot behaviour (default ALWAYS_OFF to avoid a boot brown-out)

notes: MULTI-INSTANCE - include this file more than once to get several strips. Each extra include MUST override st_led_strip_id, st_led_strip_name AND st_led_strip_pin; duplicate ids or shared data pins are errors.

POWER CAP - a WS2812-class LED draws ~60 mA at full white. A 500 mA USB budget minus ~150 mA for the ESP32 leaves headroom for only ~5-8 LEDs at 100%. st_power_limit defaults to 0.5 (color_correct halves every channel). Raise it toward 1.0 ONLY when the strip has its own injected power supply.
RGB ORDER - the WS2812/SK6812/APA106 family is GRB; WS2811 strips are commonly RGB. If red and green look swapped, flip st_rgb_order.
Effects are compiled in but cost nothing until activated from HA - the curated list below is free to keep.
rmt_symbols / use_dma are optional RMT tuning knobs; the defaults are fine for typical strips on ESP-IDF, so they are omitted here.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"
  st_led_strip_id: st_led_strip
  st_led_strip_name: "LED Strip"
  st_num_leds: "30"
  st_led_chipset: WS2812
  st_rgb_order: GRB
  st_power_limit: "0.5"
  st_light_restore_mode: ALWAYS_OFF

light:
  - platform: esp32_rmt_led_strip
    # id/name/pin are vars so the file can be included multiple times.
    id: ${st_led_strip_id}
    name: "${st_name_prefix}${st_led_strip_name}"
    pin: ${st_led_strip_pin}
    num_leds: ${st_num_leds}
    chipset: ${st_led_chipset}
    rgb_order: ${st_rgb_order}
    restore_mode: ${st_light_restore_mode}
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}
    # THE power limiter: scales every channel by st_power_limit so full white
    # can never exceed the USB current budget. 3 entries = R, G, B.
    # Block-list form (not [a, b, c]) because a ${...} at the start of a flow
    # sequence is misread as a YAML flow map by the parser.
    color_correct:
      - ${st_power_limit}
      - ${st_power_limit}
      - ${st_power_limit}
    effects:
      - addressable_rainbow:
          name: Rainbow
          speed: 10
          width: 50
      - addressable_color_wipe:
          name: Color Wipe
          add_led_interval: 100ms
          reverse: false
      - addressable_scan:
          name: Scan
          move_interval: 100ms
          scan_width: 1
      - addressable_twinkle:
          name: Twinkle
          twinkle_probability: 5%
          progress_interval: 32ms
      - addressable_random_twinkle:
          name: Random Twinkle
          twinkle_probability: 5%
          progress_interval: 32ms
      - addressable_fireworks:
          name: Fireworks
          update_interval: 32ms
          spark_probability: 10%
          use_random_color: false
          fade_out_rate: 120
      - addressable_flicker:
          name: Flicker
          update_interval: 16ms
          intensity: 5%

templates.title: PWM Dimmable Light (LEDC)
templates.description: A single-channel dimmable LED or single-color strip driven by an ESP32 LEDC PWM output. Duty-cycle cap keeps a MOSFET-driven strip inside the USB power budget by default.

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

vars.name: st_pwm_light_id
vars.description: Entity id - MUST be overridden on every extra include (see notes)

vars.name: st_pwm_light_name
vars.description: Entity name (after st_name_prefix)

vars.name: st_pwm_pin
vars.description: GPIO driving the LED/MOSFET gate

vars.name: st_pwm_frequency
vars.description: LEDC PWM frequency (ESP-IDF LEDC default is 1kHz)

vars.name: st_pwm_max_power
vars.description: 0.0-1.0 duty-cycle cap via the output's max_power - THE power limiter

vars.name: st_pwm_restore_mode
vars.description: Boot behaviour (default ALWAYS_OFF to avoid a boot brown-out)

notes: MULTI-INSTANCE - include this file more than once to get several PWM lights. Each extra include MUST override st_pwm_light_id, st_pwm_light_name AND st_pwm_pin; duplicate ids or shared pins are errors. The LEDC output id is derived as ${st_pwm_light_id}_out, so overriding the light id keeps the output id unique too.

POWER CAP - st_pwm_max_power caps the LEDC duty cycle via the output max_power (0.8 = 80% of full brightness). For a single onboard LED this is just headroom, but for a MOSFET-driven single-color strip it is the real current limiter: size the cap to your supply. A short WS2812-equivalent single-color run at ~60 mA/LED will exceed a 500 mA USB budget past ~5-8 LEDs at full duty. Raise toward 1.0 only with an external supply.
LEDC has no per-platform max_power key; max_power is a base float-output option and is the correct place to cap duty.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"
  st_pwm_light_id: st_pwm_light
  st_pwm_light_name: "PWM Light"
  st_pwm_frequency: 1000Hz
  st_pwm_max_power: "0.8"
  st_pwm_restore_mode: ALWAYS_OFF

output:
  - platform: ledc
    # id concatenates the light-id var textually, so it stays unique per include.
    id: ${st_pwm_light_id}_out
    pin: ${st_pwm_pin}
    frequency: ${st_pwm_frequency}
    # THE power limiter: caps the PWM duty cycle to keep current in budget.
    max_power: ${st_pwm_max_power}

light:
  - platform: monochromatic
    id: ${st_pwm_light_id}
    name: "${st_name_prefix}${st_pwm_light_name}"
    output: ${st_pwm_light_id}_out
    restore_mode: ${st_pwm_restore_mode}
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}
    effects:
      # Slow breathing preset that ramps between dim and bright.
      - pulse:
          name: Slow Pulse
          transition_length: 1s
          update_interval: 2s
          min_brightness: 10%
          max_brightness: 100%
      - strobe:
          name: Strobe
      - flicker:
          name: Flicker
          alpha: 95%
          intensity: 15%

templates.title: RGB Light (3x LEDC PWM)
templates.description: A common-anode/cathode analog RGB light built from three ESP32 LEDC PWM outputs (one per color channel). Per-output duty caps keep it inside the USB power budget by default.

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

vars.name: st_rgb_light_id
vars.description: Entity id - MUST be overridden on every extra include (see notes)

vars.name: st_rgb_light_name
vars.description: Entity name (after st_name_prefix)

vars.name: st_rgb_pin_r
vars.description: GPIO driving the RED channel

vars.name: st_rgb_pin_g
vars.description: GPIO driving the GREEN channel

vars.name: st_rgb_pin_b
vars.description: GPIO driving the BLUE channel

vars.name: st_rgb_frequency
vars.description: LEDC PWM frequency for all three channels (ESP-IDF LEDC default is 1kHz)

vars.name: st_rgb_max_power
vars.description: 0.0-1.0 duty-cycle cap applied to all three outputs - THE power limiter

vars.name: st_rgb_restore_mode
vars.description: Boot behaviour (default ALWAYS_OFF to avoid a boot brown-out)

notes: MULTI-INSTANCE - include this file more than once to get several RGB lights. Each extra include MUST override st_rgb_light_id, st_rgb_light_name AND all three pins; duplicate ids or shared pins are errors. Output ids are derived as ${st_rgb_light_id}_r/_g/_b, so overriding the light id keeps them unique.

POWER CAP - st_rgb_max_power caps the duty cycle on every channel via the output max_power (0.8 = 80%). With all three channels at full duty a MOSFET-driven RGB strip can pull well past a 500 mA USB budget after only a handful of LEDs (~60 mA/LED at full white). Raise toward 1.0 only with an external supply.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"
  st_rgb_light_id: st_rgb_light
  st_rgb_light_name: "RGB Light"
  st_rgb_frequency: 1000Hz
  st_rgb_max_power: "0.8"
  st_rgb_restore_mode: ALWAYS_OFF

output:
  - platform: ledc
    id: ${st_rgb_light_id}_r
    pin: ${st_rgb_pin_r}
    frequency: ${st_rgb_frequency}
    max_power: ${st_rgb_max_power}
  - platform: ledc
    id: ${st_rgb_light_id}_g
    pin: ${st_rgb_pin_g}
    frequency: ${st_rgb_frequency}
    max_power: ${st_rgb_max_power}
  - platform: ledc
    id: ${st_rgb_light_id}_b
    pin: ${st_rgb_pin_b}
    frequency: ${st_rgb_frequency}
    max_power: ${st_rgb_max_power}

light:
  - platform: rgb
    id: ${st_rgb_light_id}
    name: "${st_name_prefix}${st_rgb_light_name}"
    red: ${st_rgb_light_id}_r
    green: ${st_rgb_light_id}_g
    blue: ${st_rgb_light_id}_b
    restore_mode: ${st_rgb_restore_mode}
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}
    effects:
      # Slow drift through random colors.
      - random:
          name: Slow Random
          transition_length: 5s
          update_interval: 7s
      - strobe:
          name: Strobe
      - flicker:
          name: Flicker
          alpha: 95%
          intensity: 15%

templates.title: RGBW Light (4x LEDC PWM)
templates.description: An analog RGBW light built from four ESP32 LEDC PWM outputs (R, G, B and a dedicated white channel). Per-output duty caps plus color interlock keep it inside the USB power budget by default.

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

vars.name: st_rgbw_light_id
vars.description: Entity id - MUST be overridden on every extra include (see notes)

vars.name: st_rgbw_light_name
vars.description: Entity name (after st_name_prefix)

vars.name: st_rgbw_pin_r
vars.description: GPIO driving the RED channel

vars.name: st_rgbw_pin_g
vars.description: GPIO driving the GREEN channel

vars.name: st_rgbw_pin_b
vars.description: GPIO driving the BLUE channel

vars.name: st_rgbw_pin_w
vars.description: GPIO driving the WHITE channel

vars.name: st_rgbw_frequency
vars.description: LEDC PWM frequency for all four channels (ESP-IDF LEDC default is 1kHz)

vars.name: st_rgbw_max_power
vars.description: 0.0-1.0 duty-cycle cap applied to all four outputs - THE power limiter

vars.name: st_rgbw_color_interlock
vars.description: Prevent RGB and W being on at once (halves worst-case current) - C++ literal "true"/"false"

vars.name: st_rgbw_restore_mode
vars.description: Boot behaviour (default ALWAYS_OFF to avoid a boot brown-out)

notes: MULTI-INSTANCE - include this file more than once to get several RGBW lights. Each extra include MUST override st_rgbw_light_id, st_rgbw_light_name AND all four pins; duplicate ids or shared pins are errors. Output ids are derived as ${st_rgbw_light_id}_r/_g/_b/_w, so overriding the light id keeps them unique.

POWER CAP - st_rgbw_max_power caps the duty cycle on every channel via the output max_power (0.8 = 80%). st_rgbw_color_interlock defaults to true: it forbids the RGB and white channels being lit simultaneously, roughly halving the worst-case current draw - an important part of the USB power story. Raise the cap toward 1.0 only with an external supply.
ESPHome's own default for color_interlock is false; this template flips it to true on purpose for power safety. Set it back to "false" if you want RGB+W mixing and have the supply for it.

templates.title: Connectivity Watchdog
templates.description: Config preset that sets the WiFi reboot_timeout, so the device restarts itself if it cannot stay associated to WiFi. Exposes no entities.

vars.name: st_wifi_reboot_timeout
vars.description: How long WiFi may stay disconnected before the device reboots (0s disables the watchdog)

notes: This is built-in ESPHome behavior - the device restarts if WiFi association is lost for the whole window. This file just makes the knob explicit and overridable.
Merge caveat - if the user's own wifi: block also sets reboot_timeout, the main config's value wins over this package.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_wifi_reboot_timeout: 15min

wifi:
  reboot_timeout: ${st_wifi_reboot_timeout}

templates.title: Home Assistant Connected
templates.description: Binary sensor that reports whether the device currently has a live connection to Home Assistant (native API or MQTT).

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

notes: Reports native-API/MQTT connection state (HA reachability), not raw WiFi association - it complements wifi_signal rather than duplicating it.
No update_interval var - the status component polls its connection internally (1s); that cadence is by design and not tunable here.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"

binary_sensor:
  - platform: status
    id: st_ha_status
    name: "${st_name_prefix}Connected"
    device_class: connectivity
    entity_category: diagnostic
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}

templates.title: WiFi Channel
templates.description: Reports the primary WiFi channel the device is currently associated on, read directly from the ESP-IDF station driver.

vars.name: st_update_interval
vars.description: How often to read the current channel

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

notes: A channel number is not a measurement, so state_class is intentionally omitted (st_state_class does not apply).

Uses esp_wifi_sta_get_ap_info directly (ESP-IDF). esp_wifi.h is NOT in scope in ESPHome lambdas by default, so this file force-includes it into main.cpp via platformio_options build_src_flags (project sources only - the framework build is untouched).
Returns NAN (state unknown) when the device is not associated.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_update_interval: 60s
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"

esphome:
  # esp_wifi.h is not included in main.cpp by any component, so force-include
  # it for the lambda below. build_src_flags only affects the project's own
  # src/ (main.cpp) and the list concatenates with flags from other templates.
  platformio_options:
    build_src_flags:
      - "-include esp_wifi.h"

sensor:
  - platform: template
    id: st_wifi_channel
    name: "${st_name_prefix}WiFi Channel"
    icon: mdi:wifi-settings
    accuracy_decimals: 0
    entity_category: diagnostic
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}
    update_interval: ${st_update_interval}
    lambda: |-
      wifi_ap_record_t info;
      if (esp_wifi_sta_get_ap_info(&info) != ESP_OK) return NAN;
      return info.primary;

templates.title: WiFi Disconnect Counter
templates.description: Counts WiFi disconnect events since boot and exposes the running total as a sensor.

vars.name: st_update_interval
vars.description: How often to publish the current disconnect count

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

notes: state_class is hardcoded total_increasing (a counter), so st_state_class does not apply here.
This file's wifi: block merges into the user's wifi config - on_disconnect lists concatenate, so the user's own on_disconnect still runs.
Counts since boot; restore_value is false to avoid NVS wear. For a lifetime count across reboots, `!extend st_wifi_disconnects_value` and set restore_value: true.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_update_interval: 60s
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"

globals:
  - id: st_wifi_disconnects_value
    type: int
    restore_value: false
    initial_value: "0"

wifi:
  on_disconnect:
    then:
      - lambda: 'id(st_wifi_disconnects_value) += 1;'

sensor:
  - platform: template
    id: st_wifi_disconnects
    name: "${st_name_prefix}WiFi Disconnects"
    icon: mdi:wifi-off
    accuracy_decimals: 0
    state_class: total_increasing
    entity_category: diagnostic
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}
    update_interval: ${st_update_interval}
    lambda: return id(st_wifi_disconnects_value);

templates.title: WiFi Quality (plain language)
templates.description: Translates RSSI into a plain-language rating (Excellent/Good/Fair/Poor/Very Poor). Carries its own internal RSSI source, so it works standalone.

vars.name: st_update_interval
vars.description: How often to sample RSSI and refresh the rating

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to the entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entity disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entity from HA/web entirely

notes: Includes a hidden wifi_signal sensor (st_wifi_quality_rssi). Including wifi_signal.yaml as well adds a second, independent RSSI read - harmless, but the dBm entity lives there.

templates.title: WiFi Signal Strength
templates.description: Reports WiFi RSSI in dBm and a derived signal-quality percentage. The two entities are coupled - the % is a copy of the dBm reading.

vars.name: st_update_interval
vars.description: How often to sample RSSI

vars.name: st_name_prefix
vars.description: Prefix prepended to every entity name

vars.name: st_disabled_by_default
vars.description: Ship the entities disabled in HA

vars.name: st_internal
vars.description: Hide the entities from HA/web entirely

vars.name: st_state_class
vars.description: state_class for both entities; set "" to opt out of HA long-term statistics

notes: The % is 2*(x+100) clamped to 0..100 - the conventional community approximation, NOT a calibrated RSSI-to-quality mapping.
templates.body:
defaults:
  st_update_interval: 60s
  st_name_prefix: ""
  st_disabled_by_default: "false"
  st_internal: "false"
  st_state_class: measurement

sensor:
  - platform: wifi_signal
    id: st_wifi_signal_dbm
    name: "${st_name_prefix}WiFi Signal dBm"
    device_class: signal_strength
    unit_of_measurement: dBm
    entity_category: diagnostic
    state_class: "${st_state_class}"
    disabled_by_default: ${st_disabled_by_default}
    internal: ${st_internal}
    update_interval: ${st_update_interval}

  # Derived percentage. copy inherits device_class signal_strength from the
  # source, so clear it explicitly - a percentage is not a dBm reading.
  - platform: copy
